rounds.name: Round 1 · 连接词
rounds.description: 转折 / 并列 / 因果 / 对比 / 让步 / 总结

tokens.v: I agree with Paul that over-consumption has become a serious problem,
I would put the blame in a different place. Claire says most people only buy what they need,
I think that is actually true of many individuals. That is

why we should stop talking about greed and start talking about the system.
Modern shopping is designed to pull people in. Fast-fashion brands drop new items every week, phones are built to slow down after two years,
every app on our screen is trying to turn our attention into a purchase.
even a person who thinks "I only buy what I need" still ends up replacing a working phone every year and a half. Each choice feels normal.
the result is huge.
yes, over-consumption is serious,
the root is the system,
the shopper.

rounds.name: Round 4 · 句法骨架
rounds.description: 整块从句 / 介词短语 / 修饰结构

tokens.v: I agree with Paul

tokens.answer: that over-consumption has become a serious problem,

tokens.v: but I would put the blame in a different place. Claire says

tokens.answer: most people only buy what they need,

tokens.v: and I think that is actually true of many individuals. That is exactly why

tokens.answer: we should stop talking about greed and start talking about the system.

tokens.v: Modern shopping is designed to pull people in. Fast-fashion brands drop new items every week, phones are built to slow down after two years, and

tokens.answer: every app on our screen is trying to turn our attention into a purchase.

tokens.v: Under this kind of pressure, even a person

tokens.answer: who thinks "I only buy what I need"

tokens.v:
still ends up replacing a working phone every year and a half. Each choice feels normal. Added up, the result is huge.
The waste shows this scale clearly. Cloth landfills, electronic waste, and shipping pollution are

tokens.answer: far too large to come from rich people alone.

tokens.v: They come from

tokens.answer: the whole way the economy keeps growing by making us turn things over.

tokens.v: So yes, over-consumption is serious, and

tokens.answer: the root is the system, not the shopper.
